summary: The executor pushes as a low-privilege propose-only bot, a separate owner identity reviews and merges, and the two never share an active credential in the same session — asymmetry that contains any executor error, hostile or honest.

rules.rule: The executor pushes as a propose-only bot — write to the repo and open a PR, with no merge, no admin, no bypass of branch protection, and no self-approval of its own PR.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Bind identity per session by injecting the bot credential into the executor process's environment before launch; do not flip a global credential per task — the owner's session runs without the token and resolves to the stored owner credential.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Before any push, verify the active identity of your own session; if it is not the expected bot, STOP, do not push, and escalate to the human — never trust an identity status pasted from another window or session.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Audit the push actor via the forge's events/activity for the branch, not just the cosmetic commit author — the commit author reading as the bot proves nothing about who actually pushed.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Scope permission relaxation to the executor's session/worktree — never machine/user-global and never committed to the repo — and keep a guard even in bypass: prompt on destructive patterns and deny reads of secret files.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: The bot credential is never written to a file — the PREP step prepares the launch surface (per-spec prompt, per-session permission settings, launch script running the auth gate) whose scripts expect the credential in the session environment, never contain it.

One executor session = one worktree = one bot identity: two executors never share a working tree, the push comes from the same machine/session that owns the worktree and carries the bot identity, and dependent executors create their worktree from the latest integration branch at launch time.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate
